results.original: Early on in matters representing clients of mine was a partner in one of the large—big eight CPA firms at the time

(2) Em dash format: Muir house style uses '--' (double hyphen, no spaces per House Style Section 9). (3) 'big eight' → 'Big Eight' — proper noun: the Big Eight was the group of eight major accounting firms; both words capitalized as a defined historical term. Verbatim rule does not apply to steno dropouts of common pronouns; witness clearly said 'I'.
results.note: 'was a partner' → 'I was a partner' claims steno dropped subject pronoun, but the correction description states this is "not a verbatim correction" and applies because "speaker clearly said 'I'" — this reasoning is circular and assumes speaker intent rather than steno error; subject pronoun drops are plausible as actual witness ellipsis in conversational speech
